_sources.fact: Stuart Cramer founded Cramerton as a model mill village in 1910-1921, changing the name from Mayesworth
Goat Island Park sits on the South Fork Catawba River; Cramer released goats there in 1910 to control vegetation
Cramerton transitioned from textile mills (last closed 1999) to a diverse economy with downtown restaurants, retail, and manufacturing

localH2: Built for Cramerton and eastern Gaston County.
localP1: Cramerton was built on vision. In 1910, textile engineer Stuart Cramer transformed a modest mill town into a model village with paved streets, modern homes, and real community. The town still sits on those foundations, along the South Fork Catawba River, where Goat Island Park draws families and walkers to a greenway and trails that follow the water. Mills moved on, but the businesses here, from downtown restaurants to family shops, still run on that same building spirit.
localP2: We build for the people who keep this town going: the restaurant in the downtown, the family shop, the service crew covering the area from Belmont to Gastonia. Every site is designed and coded around your business, not stamped out of a template.
mapMetaLine: Serving Cramerton, Belmont, McAdenville, Gastonia, Mount Holly & the Charlotte metro
